content.problemStatement:
In the Chefland Library, there is a penalty system for late book returns with the following rules:


For each book returned late, penalty points equal the number of days the book is overdue.
When multiple books are returned on the same day, only the highest penalty among those books is counted.
Chef has borrowed NNN books from the library. For the ithi^{th}ith book (1≤i≤N)(1 \leq i \leq N)(1≤i≤N):


It was returned iii days late.
It was returned on day AiA_iAi​.
Calculate the total penalty points Chef must pay.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the number of books Chef borrowed from the library.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers AiA_iAi​, denoting the day on which ithi^{th}ith book was returned.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the total penalty points Chef must pay.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

On day 111, Chef returned one book which was 111 day late. Penalty point for this book is 111.
On day 222, Chef returned one book which was 222 days late. Penalty points for this book is 222.
On day 333, Chef returned one book which was 333 days late. Penalty points for this book is 333.

Thus, total penalty points are 666.
Test case 222:

On day 111, Chef returned two books. Highest penalty amongst these is 222.
On day 222, Chef returned two books. Highest penalty amongst these is 444.

Thus, total penalty points are 666.
Test case 333:

On day 111, Chef returned three books. Highest penalty amongst these is 444.
On day 222, Chef returned one book, which was 333 days late. Penalty for this book is 333.

Thus, total penalty points are 777.
